title: NIGELLA SATIVA
description: Nigella sativa, commonly known as black cumin or black seed, is a medicinal herb containing the potent bioactive compound thymoquinone, which exerts anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. It is primarily used as a dietary supplement to support immune health, manage metabolic disorders like diabetes, and reduce chronic inflammation.

research.title: Black Cumin (Nigella sativa L.): A Comprehensive Review on Phytochemistry, Health Benefits, Molecular Pharmacology, and Safety
research.summary: A broad review detailing the plant's therapeutic potential against chronic illnesses including cardiovascular disorders, diabetes, and inflammatory conditions via its rich bioactive profile.

research.title: Black Seed (Nigella sativa): A Favourable Alternative Therapy for Inflammatory and Immune System Disorders
research.summary: This review confirms that black seed modulates key inflammatory mediators like TNF-α and NF-kB, making it a viable therapy for immune-dysregulation and inflammatory disorders.

research.title: Nigella sativa: A Dietary Supplement as an Immune-Modulator on the Basis of Bioactive Components
research.summary: A systematic study identifying new bioactive compounds in the seeds that contribute specifically to its antibacterial and immune-regulating properties.

research.title: Comparative effects of nutraceuticals on body weight in adults with overweight or obesity: A systematic review and network meta-analysis
research.summary: A meta-analysis of 111 trials ranking Nigella sativa among effective nutraceuticals for weight loss and body mass index reduction in adults.

research.title: Effect of Nigella sativa oil supplement on risk factors for cardiovascular diseases in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus
research.summary: Clinical evidence showing that black seed oil supplementation improves glycemic control and reduces cardiovascular risk factors in diabetic patients.

sentiment.summary: Users widely report significant benefits for mood stabilization, anxiety reduction, and seasonal allergy relief, with many considering it a staple for general well-being.

effects.label: Clinical Consistency:
effects.description: Metanalyses confirm its efficacy for weight loss, glycemic control in diabetics, and asthma management.

effects.label: Viral Clearance:
effects.description: Research suggests it may expedite the clearance of viral infections when paired with honey or taken as an oil.

effects.label: Weight Management:
effects.description: Ranked as one of the more effective natural supplements for reducing BMI in overweight populations.

effects.label: Nootropic Potential:
effects.description: Some users find it effective for focus and managing mild ADHD symptoms when stacked with other adaptogens.

dosage.label: Standard Capsule Dose:
dosage.description: Commonly taken as 500mg capsules, often three times daily for general health.

dosage.label: Liquid Oil Protocol:
dosage.description: Users suggest starting with 1/2 teaspoon twice daily, gradually increasing to a full tablespoon.

dosage.label: Therapeutic Window:
dosage.description: Most research focuses on doses between 1-3 grams of powder or 2-5ml of oil daily.

dosage.label: Stacking Timing:
dosage.description: Often taken in the afternoon (3-4 PM) to bridge the gap between morning energy boosters and evening relaxation aids.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Upset:
sideEffects.description: Some users report 'black seed burps' or mild stomach discomfort when taking the liquid oil form.

sideEffects.label: Contact Dermatitis:
sideEffects.description: Topical application of the oil can cause allergic skin reactions in sensitive individuals.

sideEffects.label: Blood Thinning:
sideEffects.description: Due to its effects on circulation, it may have mild anti-coagulant properties that require caution before surgery.

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: May inhibit CYP3A4 enzymes, potentially slowing the metabolism of certain medications like benzodiazepines.

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Widely available as an over-the-counter dietary supplement in oil, capsule, and raw seed form.

availability.label: Quality Variation:
availability.description: Users emphasize the importance of high-quality 'cold-pressed' oils to ensure thymoquinone content is preserved.

availability.label: Global Tradition:
availability.description: Primarily sourced from Egypt, Turkey, and India, where it has deep historical use in traditional medicine.
